evidence.role: Former employee - Go to Market Lead
evidence.text: Good pay, worklife balance, and benefits Great pay and benefits

Exceptional Perks & Benefits: Free food, gyms, great healthcare, and other amenities significantly boost employee well-being. Innovation & Impact: Work on cutting-edge projects with potential to affect billions, alongside brilliant minds. Collaborative Culture: An open, inclusive environment encourages idea sharing and teamwork. High Compensation: Competitive salaries and stock options are standard, especial...
Lots of growth Good pay Lots of facilities Good pay, growth and exposure
Great benefits, food, gym, coffee, snacks - all ways to keep you working in the office Hard to be promoted
Good $01k plan and Insurance Benefits
Great work-life balance Smart, kind co-workers Amazing benefits Great work-life balance, but can be slow-moving
Good work life balance and benefits Good work life balance

Open culture, they have been adding more hierarchy but still you can have an open conversation with directors or VPs. Work life balance still though that has been changing a bit. A lot more pressure, still very flexible and open work cultures compare to other techs
Job performance and work life balance depends on the product area. Some demand a lot (devices or Cloud), whereas others (Ads) remain the growth engine. PA Dependent
Amazing food, flexibility and engineering resources to work at scale. Great place for Work-Life Balance
Good place for growth and learning Great for career growth

evidence.role: Current employee - Senior Staff Engineer, Software
evidence.text: Name recognition though these days no one seems to care about Google brand. Decent comp if you work in the premium areas like Bay or NYC. Good benefits. Political nonsense; Toxic leadership
- Lots of scope and high impact projects in the AI space - Fast moving for big tech on my team - Lots of ownership for senior level folks given the right manager Fast paced, lots of ownership

Cutting-edge projects: You get to work on products used by billions. The scale and impact are unmatched. Brilliant colleagues: The hiring bar is high, so youâre surrounded by smart, passionate people who push you to grow. Outstanding benefits: From free gourmet meals and onsite wellness centers to generous parental leave and 401(k) matchingâthe perks are industry-leading. Flexibility & autonomy: Managers gen...

Free food, and Access to Google offices and infrastructure Technically Interesting and Highly Impactful projects Great team mates and organization Massage chair and games room Great project visibility, opportunities to meet high level Google Execs like, Jeff Dean [SVP], James Manika [SVP] and Yossi Matias (VP] Life As An African Research Engineer

evidence.text: General / Software Review Pros Easy to use and intuitive interface Saves time by streamlining daily tasks Reliable performance with minimal issues Helpful features that improve productivity Good value for the price More Professional Tone User-friendly design with a short learning curve Efficient tools that simplify workflows Strong reporting and tracking capabilities Consistent performance and dependable results Res...
Team Player and good work environment. Colleagues very nice and will help each other. 1
Great work environment, nice challenges Great work environment
Positive Culture and Deeptech is interesting Positive Culture
Company has good benefits and perks Role eliminated
Despite the politics, it was great working with smart people! Smart people, but too much politics.
Safe Steady pay Free lunch and dinner Safe company
10/10 WLB and benefits, office, culture , etc Safe
I think the pros are already known the flexibility to work from anywhere the food and the benefits. Really awesome work culture!
Somewhat stable job and still lots of smart people to work with. It's changed but still a good job
stressful and management issues at times good place but stressful
There aren't any cons for this employer Good benefits
Commissions are hard to make going door to door. Benefits are awesome pay is not.

However, the differentiating culture of Google that had attracted so many initially is largely gone. The goodwill is no longer there, people are stressed and competitive about the next role, and there's probably not much to differentiate it now from any other tech company. Upward mobility is limited. Benefits/Pay remain competitive, but Culture has declined
Too much work for too little time. Everything is rushed Burnout
Managers and tech leads escaping real responsibility and blaming juniors. Hiring and promoting only those from the same regions Blatant nepotism and dirty politics

Toxic female-dominated department no clear communication When I ask nicely and politely for some clarification, I got yelled at. There was bullying involved. Lots of drama filled gossip and toxicity going on with the female clerks and heard real stories that previous staff left after few days without returning the lanyard due to toxicity and trauma Pay is not high for a legal graduate. Bullying and toxic environment
Toxic work culture esp in cloud teams Good perks and salary. Culture is toxic in some teams

Google was once one of the best companies to work for but now it's probably one of the worst. Toxic work culture and managers who have no boundaries. Managers call their reportees in the middle of the night and during weekends on their cell phone. Meetings that are supposed to be an hour long, frequently continue for 2-2.5 hrs on demand of any of the senior leaders. Barely any break during the day to even eat or go...

Increasingly political Can be incredibly toxic More and more people are focused on self-promotion by backstabbing versus excellent work Bureaucratic and slow; lack of decision makers Is no different from other big tech
1) Top heavy management. Individual contributors are less. 2) Slow growth. Good Work life balance.
* Getting promoted can be difficult, and involves a lot of politics * En-route to IBM-fication Not as exciting and innovative as it used to be
Poor Management Not fully remote Decent job
Work life balance was not very good. Google
No work life balance, too many meetings Nice place to work at
Pay is not good. They only promote if they like you No growth opportunity
No opportunity to move up, Contract meant we were stuck at a base pay rate and no possibility of a raise. No potential for growth

Slow growth and slow learning. Frequent re-orgs, project changes. Work moves slowly. Don't pay top of the band anymore -> there are better companies out there in terms of pay, which offer similar benefits & perks along with good WLB. Coaster's paradise, growth hunter's vice
No great path to career growth; overly stressful for what it is Alright
Not a lot of growth opportunity in my area Good Company
Not enough creator support Ad revenue pays basically nothing Creating is fun but ad revenue simply isnt enough
